meaning: adj.无法回答的,没有责任的,无可辩驳的
examples: They would ask their mother unanswerable questions.
The argument for recruiting McGregor was unanswerable.
They presented an unanswerable case for more investment.
Whether certain aspects of the literature are intended to mislead the outside world is equally unanswerable.
The questions he asked were unanswerable, at least by us, and he was not expecting answers, in any case.
